Title: Items
Summary: Weapons, consumables, quest items, upgrades, misc. One file per item, keyed by Slug.
Body:
## The shape

ItemData carries Slug (unique key), DisplayName, Description, Icon, IsStackable, MaxStack, Price, Category. The Category enum (`Misc` / `Weapon` / `QuestItem` / `Upgrade` / `Consumable`) groups the list page.

QuestItemData is a polymorphic subclass that also carries QuestId and CanDrop. Its constructor forces Category=`QuestItem`, IsStackable=false, Price=0. In Bleepforge JSON, the Category field is the discriminator.

## Where the file is

Godot side, items live anywhere under `shared/items/data/` (recursively). The runtime indexes them by Slug. Bleepforge's JSON cache puts them at `data/items/<slug>.json`.

## What to do here

Open [Items](/items), click a card, and edit. The Icon picker uses the [AssetPicker](/help/assets-and-image-editor/click-vs-right-click) so you can click through your project to find a texture. Slug is locked once saved (changing it would orphan every reference); to rename, delete and recreate.

> tip: there is no MaxStack slider because a real item ships with `MaxStack=10000000000` for stack always behavior. A finite cap slider would silently clip that.
